steps: Copy the Avatar_Sky_<version>.img file to the root of the VTX's SD card.
Insert the SD card into the VTX.
Power on the VTX and wait for the LEDs to settle — the upgrade runs automatically.
Do not power off the unit until the boot LEDs return to their normal pattern.
After the first successful boot, check the OSD for the new firmware version.
Copy AvatarMini_Sky_<version>.img to the Mini VTX's SD card root.
Insert the SD into the VTX.
Power on and wait until the status LED stops blinking.
Verify the new firmware version in the OSD after reboot.
Copy AvatarMoonlight_Sky_<version>.img to the Moonlight SD card root.
Connect the JST jumper cable on the unit.
Power on the Moonlight and wait for the LEDs to stabilize.
Hold the unit's button for approximately 8 seconds to initiate the update.
For a DVR-board-only upgrade, additionally create an empty file named 'independ_upgrade.txt' in the SD root (the app can do this for you).
Copy Avatar_Gnd_<version>.img to the goggles' SD card root. Delete any older firmware first.
Insert the SD card into the goggles.
Power on the goggles. The upgrade prompt appears on the display.
Confirm the upgrade and keep the goggles powered on until it reports success.
Copy AvatarX_Gnd_<version>.img to the Goggles X SD card root. Do not rename it.
Delete any older .img firmware files from the SD root.
Insert the SD card into the Goggles X.
Power on the goggles. The upgrade prompt appears on the HUD — confirm it.
Wait for the 'Upgrade success' message before powering off.
Never flash firmware below 38.44.13 on a Goggles X manufactured since Jan 2025 — it is a permanent brick.
Copy AvatarLite_Gnd_<version>.img to the Goggles L SD card root.
Insert the SD card into the goggles.
Power on and accept the on-screen upgrade prompt.
Keep the unit powered until the upgrade success screen appears.
Copy AvatarSE_Gnd_<version>.img to the VRX's SD card root.
Insert the SD into the VRX.
Power the VRX on and wait for the LED pattern to indicate a successful upgrade.
Copy AvatarMini_Gnd_<version>.img to the Recon HD SD card root.
Insert the SD card into the Recon HD.
Power on and wait for the upgrade prompt.
Confirm and keep the unit powered until the success screen.
